## [00:00 - 01:55]

You want to know how I got these scars? My father was a drinker and a fiend. And one night he goes off crazier than usual. Pitch and melody. These are the different notes we can hear. We've got all sorts of people in our lives that have different melodies that live underneath their voice. You know, you get to work and... It's Monday. It's Monday. It's Monday! I'll see you in my office in five minutes. It's the melody that lives underneath their voice. There's a very powerful strategy to enhance the melodic range in our voice. You read out a book out loud and you use the siren technique. You start with the low pitch and you go, subtext is the concept that arises often in the film of criticism. It refers to the hidden message that a film conveys to the audience beneath the surface of the story. And it tells the text. And the subtext is literally the underlying message. And then after that I come back down with my melody. What this does is it helps you improve the melody range. This is why when I speak every now and then I can go into a really high pitch and it's natural for me because I'm familiar with it. And if you don't do it right now, you're just unfamiliar for you. That's all. By me doing these, I come across vibrant. I come across as charismatic. It's funny how we perceive people to be these things when they play with their instrument a certain way. And one night he goes off crazier than usual. He turns to me and he says, why so serious, son? He comes at me with the night and he's like, why so serious? Give him a big round of applause.
